Gracy Woods Nursing Center

CCN: 675918 · Austin, TX 78758 · Travis County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data
⚠ Special Focus Facility ℹ Hospital-based

Overview

Address
12021 Metric Blvd, Austin, TX 78758
Phone
5122283300
Certified beds
118
Avg daily residents
92 (78% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2002-06-17
Setting
Urban
—
Rating not available

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Footnote 18.
— Not Available

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.82 3.15 4.68 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.18 0.55 0.22 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.16 0.70 1.42 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.48 1.90 3.04 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.34 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.15
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.10
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.86
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
56.7%
annual
RN turnover
70.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.12
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.81
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-07-31 9 5 4 143 1 143
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2025-02-28 42 24 41 1,077 1 1,077
Total weighted health score
376.5
lower is better
Infection-control citations
1
last cycle

Deficiencies (56)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
10 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-11-24 2025-11-25
0728 Ensure that nurse aides who have worked more than 4 months, are trained and competent; and nurse aides who have worked less than 4 months are enrolled in appropriate training. E 2025-11-24 2025-11-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-09-13 2025-09-15
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2025-07-31 2025-08-01
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-07-31 2025-08-01
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-07-31 2025-08-01
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-07-31 2025-08-01
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-07-31 2025-08-01
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-07-09 2025-07-10
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0914 Provide bedrooms that don't allow residents to see each other when privacy is needed. D 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. F 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. F 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0945 Include as part of its infection prevention and control program, mandatory training that includes written standards, policies, and procedures for the program. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0946 Provide training in compliance and ethics. F 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. E 2025-02-28 2025-03-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-07-26 2024-07-27
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-05-20 2024-05-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-05-16 2024-05-17
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. K 2024-05-16 2024-05-17
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. K 2024-05-16 2024-05-17
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-04-01 2024-04-02
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-04-01 2024-04-02
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-04-01 2024-04-02
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2024-03-20 2024-03-21
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. K 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. K 2024-01-26 2024-01-27
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. K 2024-01-26 2024-01-17

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
